conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and still lives at home with me. I'm wondering if I can still claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's in sixth form at the moment, doing his A-levels. He decided to stay on after his GCSEs to continue studying.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. I need to know a bit more about when Alex started this sixth form education.

What date did Alex start his current A-level course in sixth form?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He started back in September, just after finishing his GCSEs the previous summer. So he's been doing his A-levels for a while now - I think it was September 2023 when he began.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Before I can make a final determination, I need to read the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I have one more important question to help me assess Alex's eligibility.

Is Alex currently working for 24 hours or more per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working that much. He just does a bit of part-time work on Saturdays to earn some pocket money, but nothing like full-time hours. His focus is mainly on his studies.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize what you've told me:
- Alex is 18 years old
- He lives at home with you
- He is in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels)
- He started his A-levels in September 2023
- He is not working 24 hours or more per week

Based on the official government guidance, I can now provide you with a determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News - You Are Eligible!

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- While Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, it continues for young people under 20 who remain in approved education or training
- Alex is 18 years old and is in approved education (sixth form A-levels)
- He lives at home with you, so you're responsible for him
- His part-time Saturday work doesn't affect his eligibility, as it's less than 24 hours per week

**Your eligibility will continue as long as:**
- Alex remains in approved education or training
- He stays under 20 years old
- He doesn't start working 24 hours or more per week

**Important note:** Please be aware that if either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but there may be a tax implication.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and currently in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is under 20 and is in approved sixth form education. Additionally, Alex lives at home with you, so you are responsible for him. He is not working 24 hours or more per week, which means his education status is not affected by paid work. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex while he continues his approved education.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and living at home. Alex is currently in approved education (sixth form A-levels) and is not working full-time. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training. The claimant meets all the necessary criteria for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
